# Transcrição: You_don't_need_to_be_happy_all_the_time

- **Fonte:** You_don't_need_to_be_happy_all_the_time
- **Duração:** 01:06
- **Idioma:** en
- **Modelo:** small

---

## [00:00 - 01:06]

I always have the mindset that I want life to be exciting. I want life to be not boring, which leads to another point in the book where you're not meant to feel happy all the ducking time. Last week, I felt a little bit of burnout. I worked really hard, then I structured a holiday and then in the middle of my holiday, I flew to London for work for four days and then I flew back to Singapore, took some work meetings. It wasn't a holiday for me. So I went from hard work to a fake holiday and then worked even harder and then came back to hard work again. I learned myself out. And in the moments where I woke up that week last week and I kind of went, oh man, I feel kind of shitty. And then when I was going through that and I read this book and that passively was top of mind, it really made me feel better because I gave myself permission to not have to feel happy that week, to not have to feel high energy that week, to not have to be inspiring that week. It's funny because this is just a book. I don't even bloody know Mark, but his book and his words gave me permission that week to not seek happiness. Whereas my brain started going, all right, then let's go down to the range. Let's go do archery, man. All right, let's get on that bike and let's do 30 minutes of this and that. And I was like, no, I'm just going to sit on the couch and watch House of Dragons. And that felt good.
